text.title: Claude API Integration Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules covering the full Claude integration surface: sync/async/streaming, tool use, vision, Extended Thinking, Prompt Caching, Batch API. Each has a statement, rationale, source.

rule.statement: Validate `response.stop_reason` in the service layer (NOT in each subagent): raise on `max_tokens`, branch on `tool_use`, return on `end_turn`.
rule.rationale: Truncation is silent. Treating a truncated response as complete corrupts downstream parsing. Tool use loop continuation must be uniform across call sites.

rule.statement: When streaming, prefer `messages.stream` with `text_stream` for plain text and explicit event-level iteration only when tool use or thinking deltas are expected; never parse partial JSON inline — accumulate `input_json_delta` and parse after `content_block_stop`.
rule.rationale: Partial JSON is not valid; inline parsing raises `JSONDecodeError`. Event format differs from OpenAI SSE so blind cross-provider parsing fails.

rule.statement: For tool use, always submit `{"role":"assistant","content": response.content}` (the full block list) before the `tool_result`, and supply `tool_use_id` matching the model's `id` on each tool result.
rule.rationale: Omitting `tool_use` blocks from history breaks role-alternation and triggers 400 on the next turn; missing `tool_use_id` makes results unmappable.

rule.statement: For vision: encode local files to base64 and place image content BEFORE text content in the user `content` array; pre-fetch URL images for production reliability.
rule.rationale: Image-first ordering empirically improves comprehension; URL-based images can return content-error blocks at inference time.

rule.statement: Never combine Extended Thinking (`thinking.type="enabled"`) with `temperature`; set `max_tokens >= budget_tokens + 4096`.
rule.rationale: The API rejects the combination; insufficient `max_tokens` truncates the answer block silently.

rule.statement: For Prompt Caching, mark a ≥1024-token stable prefix with `cache_control:{"type":"ephemeral"}` and keep ALL dynamic content (timestamps, user ids) AFTER the cached block; monitor `usage.cache_read_input_tokens / usage.input_tokens` and assert >0.70.
rule.rationale: Prefix is byte-identical match. Drift before the cached block invalidates the cache; below ~70% hit ratio the 25% write surcharge erodes the 90% read savings.

rule.statement: For Batch API: poll at intervals ≥60s using `client.messages.batches.retrieve` until `processing_status == "ended"`; iterate results and resubmit `result.type == "errored"` items; use `custom_id` equal to your DB primary key.
rule.rationale: Tight polling adds zero speedup; errored items are not auto-retried; `custom_id` is the only join key against out-of-order results.
